accurate.why: All claims match the bill text and verifiable context; the program is the SAMHSA youth prevention and recovery initiative under 42 U.S.C. 290bb-7a(c).

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated or contradictory claims; the funding figures, statute citation, dates, and committee referrals all match the bill text.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the bill reauthorizes a youth prevention and recovery initiative by amending the SUPPORT for Patients and Communities Act.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Identifies that the bill amends Section 7102(c)(9) of the SUPPORT for Patients and Communities Act (42 U.S.C. 290bb-7a(c)(9)) by replacing the authorization of appropriations.

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill contains no material exceptions, conditions, or carve-outs to summarize.

effective_dates.why: Correctly notes the funding applies to fiscal years 2026 through 2030 and the June 3, 2025 introduction date.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the sole operative effect—replacing the appropriations authorization with new escalating funding levels for FY2026–2030.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and factual with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: The summary is well-organized, internally consistent, and reads clearly.

concise.why: The summary is appropriately brief with no padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names the Act, the statutory citation, and the two House committees of referral.

correct_figures.why: All five fiscal-year amounts ($10M, $12M, $13M, $14M, $15M for FY2026–2030) and the introduction date match the bill text exactly.
